title: The Orphan Branch
subtitle: Create a branch with no shared history

briefing.narrative:
The Head Monk has a vision.

"The monastery needs documentation. Not IN the app. Not NEXT to the app. A completely separate... dimension of documentation."

He stares into the distance like a pirate captain spotting land after months at sea. "A branch that shares NOTHING with main. No common ancestor. No shared files. Like a ghost ship sailing alongside us but in a completely different ocean."

The cat looks confused. Honestly, same.

"Some branches serve a completely different purpose than the main code. GitHub Pages uses this pattern — a branch called 'docs' that contains only documentation, with zero connection to the source code history."

He pauses dramatically. "Create me an orphan branch. A branch born from the void itself."
briefing.concept:
Sometimes you need a branch that has absolutely no relationship to your main codebase — like a GitHub Pages branch for documentation. You can achieve this by creating a branch, removing all tracked files, and starting fresh.

The key insight: not all branches need to share history. Some branches serve completely different purposes.
objectives: Remove all application files from the docs branch
Create documentation files on the docs branch
The docs branch should have no app files

args.content:
=== Monastery Application ===
Version: 3.0
Status: running
Modules: meditation, tea, garden

hints.text: A monk suggests: 'Create the docs branch with git branch docs, then git checkout docs. Remove the app files with git rm, then create your documentation.'
The cat scribbles: 'git branch docs && git checkout docs, then git rm app.txt config.txt, then create README.txt with your docs, git add README.txt, git commit.'
The Ancient Scroll: git branch docs, git checkout docs, git rm app.txt, git rm config.txt, write README.txt with documentation, git add README.txt, git commit -m "Add documentation".
